description: Headache is a common condition often triggered by stress, eyestrain, dehydration, or changes in sleep. Symptoms may include throbbing or dull pain in the head, sensitivity to light or sound, or tension across the forehead.
remedy:
• Rest in a quiet, dim room
• Stay hydrated
• Use a cold compress or massage your temples
• Over-the-counter analgesics like paracetamol or ibuprofen may help (if safe for you)
• Avoid prolonged screen time (Do: Take breaks; Don't: Skip meals or rest)

description: Fever is an elevation in body temperature, usually caused by infection. Accompanying symptoms may include chills, sweating, muscle aches, and fatigue.
remedy:
• Drink plenty of water or fluids with electrolytes
• Rest well and sleep
• Use a lukewarm sponge bath or lukewarm fluids for comfort
• Take paracetamol to reduce fever, avoiding aspirin in children (Do: Monitor temperature; Don't: Overdress or keep the room too warm)

description: The common cold is a viral infection of the nose or throat. Symptoms often include sneezing, runny or blocked nose, sore throat, mild cough, and sometimes a mild headache or fatigue.
remedy:
• Rest and stay hydrated
• Use saline nasal drops or steam inhalation to relieve congestion
• Ginger-lemon-honey tea or honey with warm water soothes throat
• Avoid cold air (Do: Wash hands frequently; Don't: Use antibiotics—they don’t work on viruses)

description: Indigestion (dyspepsia) is discomfort or burning in the upper abdomen, often occurring after meals. It may feel bloated or gassy.
remedy:
• Eat smaller, bland meals slowly
• Avoid spicy, fried, or acidic foods
• Drink warm water, fennel tea, or use fennel seeds after meals
• Avoid lying down immediately after eating (Do: Eat upright; Don't: Skip meals or overeat)

description: Fatigue is a feeling of physical or mental exhaustion that can result from overwork, inadequate sleep, stress, or underlying illness.
remedy:
• Ensure 7–9 hours of quality sleep
• Maintain a balanced diet with iron-rich foods
• Manage stress through light exercise or relaxation
• Limit caffeine late in the day (Do: Stay active; Don't: Overexert yourself)

description: Diarrhea is passing loose or watery stools multiple times per day. Common causes include viral or food-borne infections, stress, or dietary issues.
remedy:
• Stay hydrated – drink water, oral rehydration solution (ORS)
• Eat bland foods like bananas, rice, toast (BRAT diet)
• Avoid dairy, greasy or spicy items temporarily
• See a doctor if it persists more than 48 hours (Do: Rehydrate; Don't: Eat junk food)

description: Allergies are immune reactions to harmless substances (allergens) like pollen, dust, or pet dander. Symptoms include sneezing, watery eyes, nasal congestion, and itching.
remedy:
• Avoid known triggers, such as pollen or dust
• Use saline nasal rinse or antihistamines if needed
• Keep windows closed during high pollen days (Do: Clean rooms; Don't: Rub your eyes)

description: Fatigue refers to a deep sense of tiredness and lack of energy that does not improve with rest.
remedy:
• Prioritize sleep and manage workload
• Stay hydrated and maintain a balanced diet
• Implement short breaks and light activity (Do: Nap if needed; Don't: Push beyond your limits)

description: Nausea is a queasy feeling often before vomiting, usually caused by infections, food poisoning, motion, or early pregnancy.
remedy:
• Sip clear fluids slowly (water, ginger tea)
• Eat mild, plain foods (crackers, toast)
• Avoid greasy or strong-smelling foods (Do: Rest upright; Don't: Force eat)
